commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: And when he had opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come and see. Opened the second seal portending wars and shedding of blood, and so he is said to have power to take away peace from the earth. (Witham) Another red horse. This red horse signifies the cruel persecutions, which the Roman emperors carried on against the Christian religion. For this end, it is said immediately after, one sat thereon, to take peace from the earth, and kill one another; for this purpose was a great sword given to him. (Andræas; Menochius; Grotius).

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: The red horse, and he that sat upon him, having a sword, signify the coming wars, as we read in the Gospel: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there shall be great earthquakes in various places. This is the ruddy horse.
